We should never manually construct JSON payloads. You have not included proper escaping logic and this will leave us vulnerable to bugs. Please use a library to construct JSON and then convert it to a string. Either Jackson or Gson can be used for this. SO has generally preferred Jackson in the past.

We should never manually construct JSON payloads. You have not included proper escaping logic and this will leave us vulnerable to bugs. Please use a library to construct JSON and then convert it to a string. Either Jackson or Gson can be used for this. SO has generally preferred Jackson in the past.
We have attempted to limit the use of DelegateExecution within SO. What has prevented the use of only BuidlingBlockExecution (our wrapper around DelegateExecution)?

Why is DelegateExecution required here? BuildingBlockExecution is our safe wrapper around DelegateExecution. If possible we should always prefer BuildingBlockExecution.
DelegateExecution is used for custom workflows, for all other BBE will be used.

I find this name very confusing. NetworkFunction suggests that this is some general domain object which should be widely used in the rest of the SO code. While this is just local, CDS-specific interface which does not even describe object but rather operation. Could you call it accordingly? i.e. CDSRequestGenerator or CDSRequestConfiguration?

This component won't be injected by autowire. it's better to create two components, 
1. GenericCDSProcessingBB implements ControllerRunnable<BuildingBlockExecution>
2. GenericCDSProcessingDE implements ControllerRunnable<DelegateExecution>
optional to use Abstract class as parent for both components. 

in the run() method, if condition won't be required and can be handled in each component.

You can use ControllerExecutionBBTestIT for some integration tests, e.g, testExecution_cdsConfigAssign_actionExecuted can be used to test pnf config-assign. Feel free to add more test cases or new test classes.

the logic here is to construct the executionObject and set in the ExecutionContext. Presume it require another service block to actually send executionObject to CDS server. IMO, sending request to CDS can be done here as well. e.g, in CdsControllerDE, it will do both construction and sending request.

I agree with Henry. I think we should go even further. Actually content of this and GeneratePayloadForCds classes are mostly switches and ifs that decide what to do based on:
- type of the scope (VNF, PNF, etc.)
- type of the execution (BuildingBlockExecution vs DelegateExecution)

Why we can't just make VnfCDSRequestProvider, VfModuleCDSRequestProvider, ServiceCDSRequestProvider implement ControllerRunnable directly? 
And all the decisions mentioned earlier could be made:
- in the understand() method of each xxxCDSRequestProvider class (to match type of the scope),
- by using proper execution type when implementing ControllerRunnable<T> (where T will be either BuildingBlockExecution or DelegateExecution)

The only "valuable" method in this and GeneratePayloadForCds classes is buildCdsPropertiesBean() which could be implemented in some utility class or in a base class for all xxxCDSRequestProviders.

------------------------
@Henry, side question - how this implementation relates to CdsControllerDE that you provided? Isn't it an overlap?

Hi Lukasz, CdsControllerDE is being used in the configurePnfResource.bpmn. the intention is to show how the new ControllerRunnable interface can be used. I talked to Rahul and he could refactor the CdsControllerDE to support generic SO-CDS for PNF configuration and upgrade, camunda DelegateExecution API based implementation.

@Henry Done
@Lukas I'll prefer to keep GeneratePayloadForCds class because of code cohesivness and loose coupling.
